course_description:
Practicum courses develop professional skills in organisational psychology. Theories and techniques are applied in organisational settings under supervision of placement supervisors and organisational psychology staff.

The practicum component of the postgraduate program is designed to develop skills in working with organisations, across a range of professional competency areas.ﾠStudents undertake organisational placements (practica) to gain knowledge, skills, and experience in an applied setting.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

course_aims: Students undertake organisational placements (practica) to gain knowledge, skills, and experience in an applied setting. The practicum experience forms one part of an integrated program to assist student progress towards generalﾠregistration with the Psychology Board of Australia.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain, discuss and cricially examine the key theoretical and knowledge-based content areas of organisational psychology related to the practicum experience
Demonstrate practical skills related to those content areas of organisational psychology which formed the theoretical/knowledge basis of practicum experience
Understand and demonstrate ethical practice in respect to the profession of psychology and specifically, within an organisational psychology context

assessment_summary.title: Mid-Point and Final Review
assessment_summary.weight: This is the most important part of the assessment
assessment_summary.due_date: 23/02/2026 - 29/05/2026; Mid-point review is due mid-way through the practicum

assessment_details.title: Practicum Supervision Logbook
assessment_details.task_description: Students must engage in and document 35 practicum supervision hours via the practicum supervision logbook. The practicum supervision logbook should detail the learning, reflections and agreed objectives discussed in supervision and be signed by the student and their assigned placement supervisor a minimum of monthly. Separate logs should be maintained for each supervisor, where more than one has been approved for placement supervision. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Directly to the Senior Psychologist (Organisational)
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Practicum Activity Logbook
assessment_details.task_description: Students must engage in and subsequently document 250 of practicum activity hours via the practicum activity logbook. The practicum activity logbook must reflect the parameters of the placement as approved in the AAS form and be signed by the student and their assigned practicum supervisor a minimum of monthly. assessment_details.title: Mid-Point and Final Review
assessment_details.weight: This is the most important part of the assessment
assessment_details.due_date: 23/02/2026 - 29/05/2026 Mid-point review is due mid-way through the practicum
assessment_details.task_description: A mid-point and final review of work performance including knowledge and skill development must be undertaken by the student and assigned supervisor approximately half-way through the placement (Mid-Point Review) and at the conclusion of the placement (Final Review). The student and supervisor must complete and sign the Mid-Point Review and Final Review documents prior to their submission to the Senior Psychologist (Organisational). The Mid-Point Review should be submitted to the Senior Psychologist promptly (within 7 days of its completion). Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Practicum Folio
assessment_details.task_description: The practicum folio is a compilation of work undertaken during the placement. The folio consists of an exemplar of client-related and evidence-based work associated with the practica which has been undertaken and thus will differ in form and content for each student. Details regarding preparation and requirement of folio reports are contained in the Practicum Manual and will be discussed during review meetings with your Senior Psychologist (Organisational). Note that there are no maximum word limits for the practicum folio – indeed, some organisational reports are quite long and complex. In the interests of fairness however, written work such as essays and reports are required to have a minimum word count of 2000 words. Students are required to submit a printed copy of the placement folio directly to the Senior Psychologist (Organisational). Once feedback has been provided, and the student has completed and resubmitted any requested amendments, the student must upload the final folio version (complete with any amendments) as a scanned PDF file via Turnitin . Please note that the final grade for the placement course will not be released to the student until the folio is successfully uploaded. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
A hard copy of the folio and all other required documentation to be submitted directly to the Senior Psychologist (Organisational). Electronic Submission Once your folio has been reviewed and you have received an email advising you that a "Pass" grade has been recommended, you will need to collect, scan and upload the completed folio (including all documentation submitted) with the following naming convention: SURNAME_Firstname_student number_course code_semester_year {for example: SMITH_Jane_S#24682468_PSYCXXXX_Semester1(or2)_201X.pdf} All students will have access to the 'Organisational Psychology Supervised Practice Program' Blackboard Community site to upload your folio/s. Please log onto: https://learn.uq.edu.au/ to access the portal under the 'Assessment' menu (on the left hand side of the screen). If your folio PDF file is too large to upload please contact Rachelle Croton on r.croton@uq.edu.au (or telephone 07 3385 4919) for assistance. *Please remember when submitting that your folio should not contain any information that may identify a client.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission

assessment_details.title: Addition of Assistant Supervisor Form
assessment_details.task_description: Students must complete an 'Addition of Assistant Supervisor' (AAS) form at the commencement of their placement in order for any subsequent practice to be approved by the University. The form must be completed and signed by the student in conjunction with their assigned supervisor(s), and submitted to the Senior Psychologist for approval within 7 days. policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Fitness to Practise Policy Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy School guidelines Your school has additional guidelines you'll need to follow for this course: School of Psychology Student Guidelines Learning activities
